question: Does the paper report HIV sequences from patient samples?
explain: The paper mentions that HIV-1 protease (PR) and reverse transcriptase (RT) regions were sequenced from patient samples. Specifically, it states that "HIV-1 RNA was extracted from plasma," and "In 106 out of 138 patients (76.8%) the PR and RT regions of HIV-1 were amplified, sequenced, and described in this study."
sentences: "HIV-1 RNA was extracted from plasma (QIAamp Viral RNA Mini Kit/QIAGEN, Qiagen GmbH), reverse transcribed, and the complementary DNA (cDNA) was used as the target for a nested polymerase chain reaction (nested PCR) of the *pol* region as previously described."; "In 106 out of 138 patients (76.8%) the PR and RT regions of HIV-1 were amplified, sequenced, and described in this study."

question: Does the paper report in vitro drug susceptibility data?
explain: The paper does not mention any in vitro drug susceptibility testing. It focuses on sequencing the HIV-1 protease (PR) and reverse transcriptase (RT) regions and analyzing transmitted drug resistance (TDR) mutations using genotyping tools.
sentences: "The TDR rate was 3.8% (4/106; CI 95%, 1.2--8.9%) (three males, two of them MSM). Only single class mutations to NRTI (M184V; T215S) or NNRTI (K103S/N) were detected."; "TDR was analyzed using the Calibrated Population Resistance--CPR tool (Stanford Surveillance Drug Resistance Mutation--SDRM). The ARV mutation susceptibility profile was analyzed by the Stanford HIV Drug Resistance Database."

question: From which countries were the sequenced samples obtained?
explain: The paper focuses on the HIV-1 epidemic in Maranhão State, northeast Brazil. It mentions that the study was conducted among patients from Maranhão State and some from Teresina, Piauí State, both of which are in Brazil.
sentences: "HIV-1-transmitted drug resistance (TDR) and genetic diversity were investigated among 106 antiretroviral (ARV)-naive patients from Maranhão State, northeast."; "A total of 138 consecutive cases of ARV-naive patients infected with HIV-1 living in Maranhão State were enrolled (January--June 2012): 97 were recruited in São Luís at the regional referral center for CD4 ^+^ cell counts and viral loads determinations (Public Health Central Laboratory/LACEN) and 41 patients were recruited in Teresina, Piauí State at the Hospital de Doenças Tropicais Dr. Natan Portela."
